title: Beneath Bloodstone
description: A cell beneath a dark temple. The chanting above grows louder and midnight is close. You have one chance to escape before the ritual begins.
author: AI

intro.passage: You wake on cold stone, wrists raw from rope. The cell reeks of old blood and damp earth. Through rusted bars, a narrow corridor stretches into darkness. Somewhere above, a slow rhythmic chanting pulses and builds. You do not know how long you have been here. The midnight hour cannot be far off.

search-cell.passage: The floor is cold and uneven. Scratched into the mortar in one corner are dozens of names - every prisoner who passed through this cell. Beneath the lowest name, a loose stone wobbles. You pry it free and find a short iron spike, left by someone who never got the chance to use it.

hear-chant.passage: You press your ear to the bars. The chanting is in an old tongue - slow and deliberate, building toward something you do not want to witness. Then, closer than expected, a hoarse whisper drifts from the cell to your left. A woman's voice, careful and low. She has been here much longer than you.

test-bars.passage: You brace your back against the stone wall and pull. The second bar from the left grinds in its socket - rust has eaten through its base. You strain until your arms burn. Then something pops. The bar tears free. You hold a length of iron and a gap wide enough to slide through sideways.

cell-unlocked.passage: The spike finds the mechanism inside the lock. The tumblers are simple iron - whoever built this place did not expect prisoners to pick locks. After a tense minute of probing, something gives with a quiet click. The door swings inward. You step into the narrow corridor, spike still in your hand.

selene-whispers.passage: Her name is Selene. She has mapped the temple by sound over three days of captivity. Below the main hall lies a crypt. Behind the farthest coffin, she says, a tunnel leads to the forest - used by the priests to bring bodies in without being seen from the road. It is your way out.

corridor.passage: The corridor is narrow and black as pitch. Damp stone walls press close on either side. The chanting above has grown more urgent. Ahead, faint torchlight bleeds under a doorway to what looks like a guard post. Another passage continues east toward a larger hall. The air carries the sweet reek of incense.

guard-post.passage: This was once a guard post. Hooks line the walls - most empty, their weapons long taken or rusted to nothing. One hook still holds a short sword, pitted with rust but solid in the grip. A map pinned to the wall shows the temple layout. A passage leads south to the crypt.

crypt.passage: Large stone coffins line the walls, sealed with iron clasps. No inscriptions, no adornments - these were not people who died with dignity. The air is cold and perfectly still. Against the far wall, one coffin has been pushed slightly aside over the years, revealing a dark gap in the stone behind it.

antechamber.passage: This is a preparation room. Hooks line the walls where ceremonial robes hang in long rotting strips. A lectern in the corner holds a book, its pages marked with diagrams. The chanting beyond the eastern door has risen to a rhythmic fever. Two doors lead further into the temple - north and east.

dark-passage.passage: The gap opens into a rough earth tunnel. The walls are damp and roots brush your face as you move forward. Cold air presses in from ahead, carrying the faint smell of pine. The tunnel slopes upward. Behind you, voices echo in the crypt - someone has noticed the moved coffin.

coffin-risk.passage: You heave a coffin lid aside and climb in, pulling it back above you. The darkness is absolute. You lie flat, arms at your sides, barely breathing. Footsteps enter the crypt. Torchlight bleeds through the seam above your face. A voice announces your cell is empty. The footsteps slow. Then they stop.

choices.label: slip out and find the passage

library.passage: Bookcases rise to the ceiling, packed with scrolls and leather-bound tomes. A long table in the centre holds handwritten notes - ritual diagrams, star charts, lists of names written in a trembling hand. Your own name is near the top of the most recent page. Beyond the shelves, a door leads to a workshop.

choices.label: head east to the main hall

altar-hall.passage: The main chamber is vast and silent except for the chanting. Black marble pillars line the walls. At the far end, a dark red altar rises on a dais, its surface stained almost black. Robed figures stand in a ring with their backs to you. They have not yet seen you enter.

altar-dais.passage: You edge forward, stepping over fallen stones, keeping to the shadows of the pillars. The chanting reaches a fever pitch. Then the nearest figure turns - and the others follow. Their eyes are pale and unseeing, as if looking through you. From behind, hands seize your arms. You have walked into the centre of the ring.

workshop.passage: Two long workbenches run the length of the room, covered in glass vessels, coiled tubing, and powders of many colours. Shelves line the walls - dried herbs, sealed jars, bundled roots. At the far end, a crystal ball on a black iron stand pulses with a dim, cold inner light that does not flicker.

phylactery-room.passage: You pass through a low door into a chamber filled with grey mist. The mist has no source and does not move. In the centre, a dark glass vessel floats at eye level, turning slowly. Something is inside it - not a flame but a presence. It pulses when you draw near, and you feel cold.

tunnel-climb.passage: The tunnel narrows as it rises. You dig your fingers into the earth and pull yourself forward. The smell of pine grows stronger. Roots give way to stones, and the stones grow lighter in colour. Then cold night air pours over your face. A ring of pale sky appears just ahead, just within reach.

forest-edge.passage: You drag yourself out of the tunnel at the base of an enormous dead tree with black bark. The eerie forest stretches in all directions, branches tangled overhead and no stars visible through them. Behind you, the fortress looms against the sky. Then a sound - footsteps crunching through dead leaves, moving toward the tree.

priestess-confronts.passage: The coffin lid is wrenched away. A tall woman in black robes looks down at you, her face perfectly calm. Her eyes are pale as a winter sky. You are not surprised, she says. None of them ever are. She holds no weapon. She does not need one. Guards fill the doorway behind her.

choices.label: dive past her into the passage

forest-escape.passage: You run. The forest closes around you - dark and cold and full of sounds you cannot name. But behind you, no pursuit comes. By dawn, you find a road. By midday, a village. You do not look back. You do not speak of the temple for a very long time.

ending.title: Into the Dark Trees
ending.flavor: Freedom smells like pine and cold air.

selene-together.passage: The footsteps belong to Selene. She found the staircase. You run together through the black trees, her hand gripping your wrist. The forest is vast and strange but it ends. By first light you reach open farmland and the smell of woodsmoke from a village hearth. You never learned her surname. It does not matter.

sacrifice.passage: The altar is cold beneath your back. The chanting rises to a roar. You look up at the black ceiling of the temple and see nothing beyond it - no sky, no stars, no mercy. The high priestess begins. You close your eyes. The stone grows colder. The sound does not stop.

ending.title: The Rite Complete
ending.flavor: The temple always claims its due.

lich-consumed.passage: Your fingers close around the vessel. The grey mist rushes inward at once, pouring through your skin. You feel your memories lifting away one by one, replaced by something older and patient and vast. Your hand lets go. Your name lets go. Something steps back from the mist in your shape and smiles slowly.

ending.title: What Remains
ending.flavor: Some doors are better left unopened.

crystal-shattered.passage: The crystal shatters with a sound like a struck bell. The light dies. In the altar hall, the chanting dissolves into screams and then into silence. You run through the dark, following the smell of pine through a passage you had not noticed before. The forest air hits you like cold water. You are out.

ending.title: Broken Light
ending.flavor: Sometimes the only escape is breaking what sustains the darkness.
